questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has led to increased economic integration among countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has promoted free trade and investment through the reduction of trade barriers.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Globalisation has led to increased economic integration through various means, including the reduction of trade barriers, which promotes free trade and investment.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has resulted in the emergence of a new world order.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The dominance of multinational corporations and the rise of international financial institutions have contributed to this shift.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The emergence of a new world order is a consequence of globalisation, characterised by the growing influence of multinational corporations and international financial institutions.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has increased economic inequality within and across countries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The concentration of wealth and power among the elite has led to a widening income gap.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Globalisation has indeed led to increased economic inequality, as the wealth and power of the elite have concentrated, widening the income gap within and across countries.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has promoted cultural homogenisation.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The spread of Western culture and values has contributed to this phenomenon.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Yes, globalisation has promoted cultural homogenisation through the spread of Western culture and values, leading to a loss of cultural diversity.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has led to the reduction of poverty.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The creation of new jobs and the increase in international trade have contributed to economic growth.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Globalisation has not necessarily led to the reduction of poverty, despite the creation of new jobs and increase in international trade, as the benefits of economic growth have not been equally distributed.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has promoted democracy and human rights.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The spread of democratic values and the emergence of civil society have contributed to this shift.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: While globalisation has promoted some democratic values and the emergence of civil society, it has not necessarily promoted democracy and human rights universally.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has increased the vulnerability of countries to external economic shocks.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The interconnectedness of the global economy has made countries more susceptible to economic crises.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Globalisation has indeed increased the vulnerability of countries to external economic shocks, as the interconnectedness of the global economy has made countries more susceptible to economic crises.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has promoted sustainable development.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The emergence of green technologies and the shift towards environmental protection have contributed to this shift.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: While globalisation has promoted some sustainable practices, it has not necessarily promoted sustainable development universally, as the pursuit of economic growth has often come at the expense of environmental protection.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has led to the decline of traditional industries.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The rise of multinational corporations and the shift towards free trade have contributed to this shift.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Globalisation has led to the decline of traditional industries, as the rise of multinational corporations and the shift towards free trade have made it difficult for local industries to compete.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Globalisation has promoted social and cultural exchange.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The increased movement of people, goods, and ideas has contributed to this phenomenon.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Yes, globalisation has promoted social and cultural exchange, as the increased movement of people, goods, and ideas has contributed to a more interconnected world.
